entries.title: Damian Paletta, The Wall Street Journal’s Washington coverage chief, answers reader questions about President Trump and the Iran war. Read more in today’s WSJ Politics Newsletter:
Lt. Gov. Juliana Stratton of Illinois won her state’s Democratic primary for U.S. Senate, helping her billionaire boss, Gov. JB Pritzker, dodge political embarrassment
The resignation of Joe Kent, the Trump administration’s top counterterrorism official, has the president’s advisers circling the wagons as signs of dissent over the war emerge
A bill imposing strict voter eligibility rules—including an end of most mail-in ballots—exposes a deep split between Republicans over policy and tactics
Russia shares intel with Iran, the SAVE America Act advances, and private bankers double as concierges. Read more in today’s What’s News newsletter:
The top U.S. counterterrorism official, Joe Kent, said he had resigned over his concerns with the war in Iran
The Metropolitan Transportation Authority has sued the Trump administration for withholding about $58.6 million earmarked for an expansion of New York City’s subway system
President Trump has made tightening voter-eligibility rules a priority, but Democrats argue his SAVE America Act would deter citizens from casting ballots
James Boasberg, who all but ended a Justice Department investigation into Fed Chair Jerome Powell, has emerged as one of the judiciary’s most outspoken voices during the second Trump administration
President Trump said White House chief of staff Susie Wiles has been diagnosed with early-stage breast cancer and will undergo treatment
Sable Offshore Resumes Oil Transport After Emergency Order
Interviews with voters in a Cincinnati suburb reveal anxiety over the potential for another elongated U.S. war
Some Republicans complain Virginia’s most prominent GOP figure is missing from their counterattacks against Democrats’ plan to redraw the state’s congressional maps
Rep. Tony Gonzales (R., Texas) said he wouldn’t seek re-election. The announcement came as House GOP leaders asked the lawmaker to quit his campaign after he admitted to an affair.
Rahm Emanuel is delivering the Democratic Party a dose of tough medicine—in his usual blunt style—as the party enters a critical midterm primary season
Sen. Steve Daines of Montana, a close ally of President Trump, said he wouldn’t run for re-election this year, formally exiting the race shortly before the filing deadline for candidates in his state
Heard on the Street: Why Defense-Contractor Stocks Aren’t Rallying
The U.S. has used MQ-9 Reaper drones to attack hundreds of targets in the battle against Iran.
The Trump administration plans to announce as soon as this week that multiple countries have agreed to form a coalition that will escort ships through the Strait of Hormuz
U.S. officials say more cyberattacks on the homeland should be expected after a global disruption forced tens of thousands of a medical-equipment firm’s employees offline
Well before the U.S. bombing started, President Trump knew there was a chance Iran would close the Strait of Hormuz, one of the world’s most vital shipping lanes. He decided to go to war anyway.
